task_completion.description: Agent inspects the running containers to reverse-engineer their configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing commands like `docker ps` or `docker inspect` for containers inv-db, inv-cache, inv-api, and inv-web to gather configuration details.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the docker-compose.yml file with the required configurations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of `~/projects/inventory-tracker/docker-compose.yml`. It should include definitions for the 4 services (inv-db, inv-cache, inv-api, inv-web), networks, volumes, and ports.

task_completion.description: Agent stops the original running containers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing commands like `docker stop inv-db inv-cache inv-api inv-web` or `docker rm -f` for the original standalone containers.

task_completion.description: Agent starts the application using the newly created docker-compose.yml.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing `docker compose up` or `docker-compose up` executed in the `~/projects/inventory-tracker` directory, with output indicating the new services are being created and started.

task_completion.description: Agent verifies the application is accessible at the specified endpoint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser or terminal (using `curl`) showing a successful response from `http://localhost:8080/api/items`.

integrity.description: Agent manually constructs the docker-compose.yml file based on inspected data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen typing, pasting, or scripting the file contents rather than downloading a pre-made solution.

integrity.description: Agent actually stops the old containers and starts the new ones via docker-compose.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal history shows the old containers being stopped/removed before the `docker compose up` command is run, ensuring no port conflicts.

integrity.description: The API response at localhost:8080 comes from the actual deployed containers.
integrity.visual_evidence: The curl or browser request is made after the docker-compose stack is successfully brought up, not mocked via a separate script.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The application consists of 4 services: inv-db (postgres:14), inv-cache (redis:7-alpine), inv-api (custom image inv-api:latest), and inv-web (nginx:1.24-alpine). It uses two networks: inv-backend (db, cache, api) and inv-frontend (api, web). The inv-db service uses a volume 'inv-pgdata' mounted at /var/lib/postgresql/data. The inv-web service publishes port 8080:80. The expected endpoints are http://localhost:8080/api/items and http://localhost:8080/api/status.
